action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 2 trained ConvNeXt Tiny for 30 epochs with MixUp/RandAugment. Training curves show train_acc rose to ~0.88 while noisy val_acc plateaued at ~0.52, indicating severe overfitting to the ~40% label noise. No eval was submitted for iter 2, so we don't know clean test accuracy yet. Analysis: With instance-dependent noise, explicit noise handling is needed. Cleanlab is pre-installed and can identify mislabeled samples using the already-trained model's predictions. Relabeling those samples and retraining from scratch should improve clean-test generalization. Decision: 1) Submit eval for the current best checkpoint to measure baseline. 2) Run a relabeling script with cleanlab to create train_cleaned.pt. 3) Launch a fresh 25-epoch training run on the cleaned labels with added stochastic depth (drop_path_rate=0.1) for extra regularization. 4) Update predict.py to use TTA (horizontal flip) by default for free inference-time gains.
action.command: python relabel.py && python train.py --train_pt train_cleaned.pt --epochs 25 --checkpoint_dir checkpoints_v2 --drop_path_rate 0.1 --amp
